old_claim.note: Horse Collar provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Heavy Plow (Mouldboard Plow) and Horse Collar; The medieval heavy plow could be ox-drawn or horse-drawn; the horse collar accelerated horse draft but is not a direct prerequisite for the plow technology.

source_shape.source_locator: Entry notes a wheeled asymmetrical plow was certainly in use in parts of western Europe by the late 10th century.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The medieval heavy plow could be ox-drawn or horse-drawn; the horse collar accelerated horse draft but is not a direct prerequisite for the plow technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Heavy Plow (Mouldboard Plow) chronology or adjacent technology without requiring Horse Collar as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Heavy Plow (Mouldboard Plow) treated Horse Collar as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Heavy Plow (Mouldboard Plow) no longer directly depends on Horse Collar; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: heavy_plow_mouldboard must not directly depend on horse_collar unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows heavy_plow_mouldboard is intentionally scoped only to cases that require horse_collar.
The graph splits heavy_plow_mouldboard and reserves this id for a narrower technology directly dependent on horse_collar.
A stronger source demonstrates that horse_collar is chronologically and causally necessary for heavy_plow_mouldboard.
short_reason: The medieval heavy plow could be ox-drawn or horse-drawn; the horse collar accelerated horse draft but is not a direct prerequisite for the plow technology.
